content.problemStatement:
Chef has three spells. Their powers are AAA, BBB, and CCC respectively. Initially, Chef has 000 hit points, and if he uses a spell with power PPP, then his number of hit points increases by PPP.

Before going to sleep, Chef wants to use exactly two spells out of these three. Find the maximum number of hit points Chef can have after using the spells.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers AAA, BBB, and CCC.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum number of hit points.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers A, B and C denoting the powers of the three spells.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum possible hit points Chef can have after using exactly two spells.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: the two largest powers are 8 and 4, sum = 12.\nTest case 2: the two largest powers are 18 and 14, sum = 32.
Case 1: all equal, any two give 2.\nCase 2: largest two are 5 and 5, sum = 10.\nCase 3: largest two are 9 and 7, sum = 16.

hidden.label: edge case with two equal maxima

solution.approach: For each test case we need the sum of the two largest numbers among A, B and C. The easiest way is to compute the total sum A+B+C and subtract the smallest of the three values. The smallest value can be found with a few comparisons or by using the built‑in min function. This yields the required maximum sum in O(1) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of input size.
